_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/Renal Transplantation/ : Renal transplantation_MEDCO.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + Renal transplantation_PPD_CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: Fellowship in Transplant Surgery with 2 years training
Equivalent qualification with 2 years training in Transplant Surgery
clinical_indications: Immunosuppressive therapy administered at the time of kidney transplantation to reduce the risk of allograft rejection.
Practically all kidney allograft recipients require immunosuppressive therapy to prevent rejection and loss of the allograft.

preauth.label: Clinical notes with planned line of management
Hospital authorization letter on recipient and donor with details of the surgery

claim.label: Details of the drug and dosage used for induction

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes all vitals, detailed history, symptoms, signs, physical examination, indication for surgery, planned line of treatment, and advice for admission?
Were the following documents available NOTTO ID, donor/recipient work-up summary sheet/cross-match report, informed consent of donor (in living donor transplant), hospital authorization letter?
Were DSA and biopsy reports submitted (if applicable)?
Is the hospital registered under HOTA/NOTTO ACT?
Has the hospital transplant committee approved the case?
Is the patient suffering from end stage kidney disease and/or not responding to dialysis?
Documentation of no absolute contraindications for doing the procedure?
Is the Donor medically fit to undergo the Transplant Donor Nephrectomy?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details (including drug and dosage for induction/treatment rejection – if applicable)?
Are the detailedprocedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Hospital not registered under HOTA/NOTTO Act.
Hospital transplant committee has not approved the case.
Patient is not suffering from end-stage kidney disease, or is responding to dialysis.
Absolute contraindications for the procedure are documented.
Donor is not medically fit to undergo Transplant Donor Nephrectomy.
Missing documentation of indication for induction at pre-authorization.
Missing hospital authorization letter on recipient and donor at pre-authorization.
Missing details of the drug and dosage used for induction at claim stage.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Facility should be registered as per HOTA Act (THE HUMAN ORGANS TRANSPLANTATION ACT)for intervention procedures. Care at a Tertiary Hospital. The Hospital should have the required infrastructure of transplantation like Normo-thermic perfusion system, vascular access, pre-transplant work-up, dialysis, transplant immunology (HLA & cross match), transplant pathology and post-transplant care.
clinical_key_pointers: High risk for rejection: Rabbit antithymocyte globulin (rATG)-Thymoglobulin is superior to interleukin (IL) 2 receptor antibodies and placebo among patients at high immunologic risk who are receiving concurrent immunosuppressive regimens. Administer rATG-Thymoglobulin, a lymphocyte-depleting agent, rather than an IL-2 receptor antagonist or no antibody therapy to high-risk patients undergoing kidney transplantation.

Lower risk for rejection: Some administer rATG-Thymoglobulin based upon evidence that it produces lower acute rejection rates. Others administer an IL-2 receptor antagonist based upon studies that have shown similar rates of acute rejection, patient and graft survival, and infection with rATG-Thymoglobulin and IL-2 receptor antagonists.
